title: teams — trait composition
description: Five owned traits. `BelongsToTeam` is the FK trait for downstream models (Wave 3 sports Session, Wave 3 attendance Visit). `HasTeamMembers` / `HasTeamTrials` / `HasEventParticipation` are inverse-side conveniences on the Team model. `PolymorphicTeamMember` is the reverse-polymorphic trait — mixed into Athlete / Coach / Staff / User so those models expose `->teams()` via a reverse-polymorphic query. Order matters when composed with `BelongsToTenant` / `BelongsToBranch` — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context in their `booted()` hooks.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `team_id` FK. Adds the `team()` belongsTo relation, a `resolveInTeam(?Team)` query scope that filters `WHERE team_id = ?` (falling back to the caller's `Request::activeTeam()` when the arg is omitted), and a `booted()` hook that fills `team_id = active_team_id()` on `creating` when the field is null AND the current request carries a resolved active team. Refuses to save a row whose `team_id` points at a Team in a different tenant than the row itself (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling).
usedBy: sports::Session (Wave 3 — practices book a Facility for a Team's slot)
attendance::Visit (Wave 3 — visit rows tie to team_id for roster filtering)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToTeam` in the trait list so the tenant scope resolves first.
`BelongsToBranch` is implied by the team — do NOT compose `BelongsToBranch` on models that already carry `BelongsToTeam` (the branch cascades through team → branch_id in queries).

The `booted()` fill hook is off by default when the consumer's create-flow always specifies team_id explicitly (TeamMember, TeamTrial, EventTeam). Turn on per-model via `bool $fillTeamOnCreating = true`.

owned.description: Composed onto the Team model. Adds `->members()` (hasMany over TeamMember), `->activeMembers()` (scope filter status='active'), `->membersByType(string $type)` (filter member_type), `->athletes()` (shortcut for membersByType('athlete')), `->coaches()` (shortcut for membersByType('coach')), `->users()` (shortcut for membersByType('user')), `->rosterCount()` (cheap cached active-count read).

Composed onto the Team model. Adds `->trials()` (hasMany over TeamTrial), `->scheduledTrials()` (scope filter status='scheduled'), `->completedTrials()` (scope filter status IN ('completed', 'converted', 'rejected')), `->pendingDecisionTrials()` (scope filter status='completed' — awaiting coach's convert/reject decision).

Composed onto the Team model. Adds `->eventTeams()` (hasMany over EventTeam), `->upcomingEventTeams()` (scope filter withdrew_at IS NULL AND (event.starts_at > now() OR event.starts_at IS NULL for Wave 2)), `->pastEventTeams()`, `->currentSeasonEventTeams()` (Wave 3 addition).

The REVERSE-POLYMORPHIC trait. Mixed into any model that CAN be a team member (Athlete, Coach, Staff, User). Adds `->teams()` — a Collection<Team> resolved via a reverse-polymorphic query on team_members where (member_type = this model's polymorphism key, member_id = this.id, status='active'). Also adds `->historicTeams()` for the terminal-state variant. The trait consults `config('teams.polymorphism.member_type_registry')` for the reverse mapping — polymorphism registration is bi-directional.
usedBy: sports::Athlete (Wave 3 — added when Sports module lands)
staff::Coach (Wave 2b — added when Staff module lands)
staff::Staff (Wave 2b — added when Staff module lands)
composition_notes: This is the ONLY reverse-polymorphic trait in the teams module. Every consumer of 'give me the teams this person is on' goes through it — never through a bespoke query.
Adding a new member_type = adding a new consumer of this trait + registering in the polymorphism registry.

framework.reason: Team, TeamMember, TeamTrial, EventTeam — all four. The retention pipeline governs hard-purge windows.

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

organization.reason: Team carries organization_id directly (structural sub-brand context). Other rows cascade through team_id.

branch.reason: Team only — carries branch_id. Other owned rows cascade through team.

audit.reason: Every write to teams / team_members / team_trials / event_teams is compliance-relevant (child-safety + anti-discrimination + financial when trial fees applied).

activity.reason: Roster changes + trial lifecycle + event participation feed the staff-visible activity feed.

team.notes: Order matters: BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. HasSlug generates the (tenant_id, branch_id)-unique slug on create. HasTeamMembers + HasTeamTrials + HasEventParticipation are inverse-side conveniences. NO BelongsToRegion — teams cascade region through branch (tenancy-columns.md §5).

team_member.notes: NO BelongsToBranch / BelongsToOrganization traits — cascade through team. Auditable applies because child-safety + anti-discrimination retention requires audit trail on every state transition. The polymorphic `member` accessor is NOT a trait — it's a method on the model itself that delegates to PolymorphicTeamMemberResolver.

team_trial.notes: Auditable is critical — decision_notes is the anti-discrimination defence record. Same polymorphic delegation for `trialist` accessor.

event_team.notes: Auditable for result-tampering audit trail. SoftDeletes with 7-year hold when financial fields populated (Wave 4).
